listitem.para: Whether or not to send the ESP_TFC_PADDING_NOT_SUPPORTED notification to the peer informing it that that this end does not support Traffic Flow Confidentiality ("TFC") (RFC-4303). Possible values are &no; (do not send notifification), which allows the peer to use TFC, and &yes; (send notification) which prevents to peer from using TFC when sending &ESP; packets.
This does not affect whether this endpoint uses TFC when sending &ESP; packets - it depends on the tfc= parameter and the peer sending sending an ESP_TFC_PADDING_NOT_SUPPORTED notification).
History: the parameter send-no-esp-tfc= was added in &Libreswan; 3.18; in &Libreswan; 5.3, the parameter was renamed to send-esp-tfc-padding-not-supported=.
The default is &no;. This option is only valid for &IKEv2;.
